title: Srilankan Sundari - Malayalam - Drama - Movies
synopsis:
Oru Srilankan Sundari in AUH

	Inara  a Srilankan lady  working as a nurse in Abudhabi brings  her daughter Hana to Abudhabi  from her  Malayali Father Ajith an Atheist who stays in a Kerala village with his paralysed mute mother. The unexpected and uninvited happenings shatters Inara’s expectations of keeping her only daughter under her wings,……. She has taken away the project papers of her friend who is studying with her and is afraid of the police and become mental disorders through  repeated  seizures. Inara was forced to handover her daughter  to Ajith to  save her from the mishaps with a broken heart. Later Ajith finds out the reasons and find solutions to save his daughter ….. finally realising the spirituality and the existence of Spiritual being..


അബുദാബിയിൽ നഴ്‌സായി ജോലി ചെയ്യുന്ന ശ്രീലങ്കൻ വനിതയായ ഇനാറ തളർവാതബാധിതയായ മിണ്ടാപ്രാണിയായ അമ്മയ്‌ക്കൊപ്പം കേരളത്തിലെ ഒരു ഗ്രാമത്തിൽ താമസിക്കുന്ന നിരീശ്വരവാദിയായ തന്റെ മലയാളി പിതാവ അജിത്ത് നിന്ന്  മകൾ ഹനയെ അബുദാബിയിലേക്ക് കൊണ്ടുവരുന്നു. മകളുടെ  അറിവില്ലായ്മകൊണ്ടു  ചെയുന്ന തെറ്റുകാരണം  തന്റെ ഏക മകളെ ചിറകിനടിയിലാക്കാനുള്ള ഇനാരയുടെ പ്രതീക്ഷകളെ തകർത്തു. കൂടെ പഠിക്കുന്ന കൂട്ടുകാരിയുടെ പ്രൊജക്റ്റ് പേപ്പർസ് എടുത്തുകളഞ്ഞു, പോലീസിനെ പേടിച്ചു മനോരോഗിയെ പോലെ ആകുന്നു . തകർന്ന ഹൃദയത്തോട സുഖമില്ലാതായ മകളെ അജിത്തിന്റെ അടുക്കൽ തിരിച്ചയക്കുന്നു. പിന്നീട് അജിത്ത് മകളെ രക്ഷിക്കാൻ കാരണങ്ങൾ കണ്ടെത്തുകയും പരിഹാരങ്ങൾ കണ്ടെത്തുകയും ചെയ്യുന്നു ... ഒടുവിൽ അജിത് ആത്മീയതയും ആത്മീയ ജീവിയുടെ അസ്തിത്വവും തിരിച്ചറിഞ്ഞു.

synopsis:
“Second Innings” follows Parth, a once-celebrated theatre prodigy now lost in the unforgiving streets of Mumbai, where dreams often die louder than they’re born. As his past glory fades and despair consumes him, Parth is forced to confront his shattered ambitions and the truth about who he’s become. When life offers him one final chance to rise again — will he take it, or let the curtain fall forever?

title: Rajgarh - Hindi Thriller - Shortfilm
synopsis:
In the seemingly ordinary act of littering, a mysterious narrative unfolds. A group of children, carefree and playful, toss trash into the air, but beneath their innocent facade lies a complex web of secrets and motives. As the camera pans out, the true story begins to reveal itself, twisting and turning in unexpected ways. What appears to be a simple act of mischief slowly unravels into a tale of intrigue, leaving the viewer questioning what lies beneath the surface.

title: Prema Gaadha - Telugu - Pilot Film - Love
synopsis:
"During 2002 to 2010 in between vintage love story.
In a small town, two 10th-grade students, Raju and Rani, are dealing with difficult family lives. Raju, a shy and introverted boy, faces the heartbreaking illness of his mother, Indramma, while struggling with a stammer. Rani, an intelligent girl, lives under the shadow of her cruel stepmother, Lakshmi, after losing her own mother. Her father, Raghu Ram Rao, is unable to protect her from the mistreatment at home.
When Rani is injured and sent to the hospital, she meets Raju for the first time. A silent connection forms between them, but Raju hides his stammer, fearing rejection. Rani, unaware of his secret, feels a growing friendship with him. As Rani’s life becomes even more difficult with her stepmother’s pressure to leave private school for a government one, Raju becomes her pillar of support.
Together, they navigate school, face personal struggles, and slowly grow closer, with Rani helping Raju break through his own fears. Amidst their emotional journey, Raju and Rani find love and healing in each other. But just when they feel they’ve overcome everything, a big secret about Rani’s past is revealed, changing the course of their relationship.
In this heartfelt story of love, friendship, and resilience, Raju and Rani prove that despite their flaws and struggles, love can be the ultimate strength to heal and bring them together."

title: Madharasil oru vanmam - Pilot Film - Tamil - Drama
synopsis:
ஒரு ஏரியாவின் வாழ்வியல்.  அதில் காதல், குடும்பம், நண்பன், உறவு, பகைவன் என்று அனைத்தையும் நேர்க்கொண்டு நகரும் ஒரு இளைஞர் கூட்டத்துக்கும், அதே பகுதியில் சதி திட்டம், ஏற்றத்தாழ்வு, காழ்ப்புணர்ச்சி, ஜாதி என்று பல கோணங்களில் சூழ்ச்சி செய்யும் அரசியல்வாதி அன்புராமனுக்கும் இடையே நடக்கும் துரோகமும் , வன்முறையுமே இப்படத்தின் கதை.

This story is set in an area where life unfolds, involving themes of love, family, friendship, relationships, and spirituality. It showcases a group of youngster navigating through various aspects of life. The story touches upon betrayal, power struggles, deceit, caste issues, and corruption. The plot revolves around a politician named Anburaman, who is involved in schemes and conspiracies, while the story also highlights the backdrop of violence and conflict.

title: Watch Ponnana Nimidangal - Starring Good night Manikandan & Balasaravanan
synopsis:
The story opens with a flashback set in the midst of a bustling wedding hall, where we first meet Sandhya. An unusual incident forges a close bond between her and Kathir, and over time, their friendship gradually evolves into love. However, Kathir hesitates to confess his feelings, worried that expressing his love might ruin their relationship and burdened by his current financial insecurities.

Adding a lighthearted counterpoint to this unfolding drama is Thiru, Kathir’s roommate. Through his lively conversations and witty exchanges, Thiru not only supports the storyline but also injects humor and perspective into the narrative.

While the film unfolds against a backdrop of diverse and picturesque locations, it follows the familiar path of a one-sided love story filled with comedy, romance, sacrifice, and deep friendship. Yet, just when the audience believes they know what to expect, an unexpected twist at the climax turns everything on its head.

The rising dreams of every child is effected in different way by the culture from their born period.

title: Kashish - Hindi Thriller Suspense Drama Shortfilm
synopsis:
'Kashish' is a well-meaning conversation on domestic abuse and how it can extend beyond simply a physical manifestation, or fixed expectations that one might have of an abused-and-abuser-equation. It attempts the same by showing a doctor's procedure of conducting an autopsy on someone who took their life by suicide. As she unravels each layer, the plot is also gradually shared with the viewer.

title: Viraama (Break) - Experimental Kannada Short Film
synopsis:
A young boy who is a chainsmoker falls into depression about the fact that he is smoking. But after changing time period at different intervals, he takes break in his life journey by showing us that how will he manipulate his mental state and choose among his upcoming different directions ahead. 
How cigarette has changed his life from time to time and how the understanding of addiction by himself changed his life? Did he use or misuse the smoking addiction? Was he acceptable for the possible directions that were ahead of him and did really his initial addiction stage transformed him to recover from that pain?

title: Covid 66 - Hindi Drama Shortfilm
synopsis:
The short film (16 minutes15 seconds) presents a young man who develops fear of covid virus infection and death during covid 19 lockdown primarily due to cyberchondriac syndrome. As the film progresses from a light comedy backdrop, he gets serious and begins feeling the covid symptoms such as loss of smell, fever and low level oxygen. He becomes his own doctor and relies heavily on the prescriptions offered by social media. During this state of fear he was utterly confused while reading the digits on oxymeter. He wrongly reads his oxygen level 99 to 66 and then gradually slips into delirium. His doctor friend at the end appears for his rescue and teaches him the importance of consulting a doctor instead of falling back on one’s own prescription.
The film conveys strong message for all those who think internet is the panacea of all problems. The isolation during the lockdown has made many mentally sick. The short film speaks volumes about the need to consult a doctor in case of such crisis.

title: Chernobog - Experimental Suspense Thriller Tamil Short Film
synopsis:
Dan is a middle aged boy who is suffering from 
Dissociative Identity Disorder. He has two personalities, one 
is Dan who is a normal middle aged boy and another is an 
unnamed psycho who lives inside him who is addicted to the 
blood's smell. The cause for Dan's disorder is by birth 
itself is. He is exposed to the blood's smell. As he grows he 
controls the emotion inside him. This gradually grows inside 
him and he develops an another personality. So whenever he is 
exposed to blood the other person comes to light and starts 
to rip off the skin on any living thing to get more blood. 
And when he gets the needed amount of blood out and smelled 
it, the psycho gets satisfied and goes inside and the normal 
Dan comes out without knowing anything what happened. He 
feels like he has came out from a hangover sleep. 
 One day on a fine evening, he feels alone and 
decides to invite a call girl to his house for company. There 
twists and turns happens, and for the first time he comes to 
know that he has an another personality inside him. In the end after knowing everything, he 
itself is exposed to blood from his own body. The story ends 
with a twist weather he would kill himself or not.

title: Agarathi - Tamil Suspense Shortfilm
synopsis:
AGARATHI is a journal of historical research about TAMIL Language. On screen three archaeologists, who were planning to find out a mysterious priest who disappears in Kanyakumari at the 770kms across the sea, On research they concluding that the Mysterious priest went to the lost continent KUMARI KANDAM…The research continuous at-last they came to know about the history of the lost continent and they decided to find out World’s first human and world’s first spoken language ‘TAMIL’…They attempted to search for the secret continent, by the unexpected magical mystery, the lost continent appears from the sea…They venture makes them inside to the KUMARI KANDAM to find out the mysterious priest, by the way...unfortunately they cause a landslide that buries and they are trapped into a cave...They continue on into the cave without a choice in hopes of finding a way out, but as they do, they hear strange sounds coming from the darkness. The shocking events and the problems that arise forms the crux of the story.

title: Norway Our Way ...A National Festival! - Real Story Shortfilm
synopsis:
Norway Our Way ...a national festival! is a loud and colourful observational experience.

More often than not, whenever Norway is mentioned in the international media it’s usually to announce that Norway - yet again - has been awarded the honour of having the best quality of life of any country in the world. What is it that makes Norway so special that it receives such frequent accolades?

As an ex-pat Norwegian who’s lived abroad for more years than I care to admit, I know the reasons why. It’s all to do with social cohesion and a shared value system that most Norwegians subscribe to. But how has this all come about?

I’ll tell you why. Every year in the middle of May - on the 17th to be precise - Norway celebrates its Constitution Day. An event when every Norwegian schoolchild, whether in the big cities or in the country towns, dresses up in their very best clothes, a small Norwegian flag in hand and marches through the streets of their home town accompanied by brass bands and indulge in a frenzy of loud and colourful patriotic fervour. This is the instrument, I believe, which binds this country together.

And I know. As a child brought up in Oslo there was always a joyous anticipation for the upcoming celebrations every spring. New clothes were obtained. Flags were dusted down and the school band rehearsed well into the night to ensure the music would be up to scratch for the big day. And of course, children are the hope of all nations: The future lies with them.

I noticed many changes when I returned to Oslo last year to celebrate 17th May and this film - made entirely by myself - is my own personal homage to this extraordinary and unique event that fosters the values that Norwegians hold dear and is perhaps the secret to their enviable quality of life.

title: Khat Ka Anand - Hindi Drama Short film
synopsis:
Today Meenal is writing a letter like every other day. The soft winds and the rains made the morning fresher and greener, though Meenal on the contrary, looks as dry as autumn leaves.
Suraj, Meenal’s other half left for work even before the henna on her hands could fade away. His work, duties, and responsibilities are inevitable. Yes, this is the story of someone’s life, serving the military.
Suraj, a brave soldier guarding the Indo-Chinese border. And 'his responsibilities', the reason why Meenal couldn’t be with her husband for a long time.
Meenal’s happiness drowned as soon as Suraj left for work. She found a way to depict her silence, it was through letters, by mentioning how largely she misses him and loves him. Meenal while staying at her mother’s, after Suraj left, began to write a letter in the present day as well, she used to say everything by writing things that were heartfelt by those moments.

Those were the times when people didn’t express love openly. Meenal’s father was a postman, she used to give those letters to him and his father would also bring some letters back while returning home, as Suraj used to write back to her wife. Telling how much he loved her. Time flew, days passed, and Meenal becomes unhappier.

Meenal’s Love poems were still in writing, but she stood in sorrow since she had no letters or replies coming from her husband. Few miserable days went by and finally, there comes a letter by Suraj, that her father brought. Meenal blossomed like a fresh lily as soon as she touched the letter and once again, the story of love letters resumed. Since Meenal now, was happier than ever, she used to express more, the most she could.
It was a dark evening, Electrical failure led to a blackout in the house. Meenal began to lighten lamps as her mother asked so. In the process, she went to her father's room and was surprised to see that her father jittered as she opened the door, he tried to hide something. 'Never seen him like this before'- thought Meenal.
For the moment, she resisted asking anything to her father and went back but couldn’t stop thinking about it.

Wondering, What was he doing?

That night, once everybody fell asleep. Meenal went to her father’s room again to discover the truth. The first thing to notice was the torn papers on the floor that her father threw. That had her address. It seemed to Meenal as if her father was trying to write letters to someone, so she started searching thoroughly.
Few seconds and Meenal was surprised to have found, that these were the same letters that she gave to her father. Before she could get out of the current shock, she found another letter over letter pad, which referred to Suraj.
It was Meenal’s father who wrote all the letters pretending to be Suraj.
She was in utter pain and started crying, meanwhile found another letter, only this time it was a real one from Indian Defence. 'Suraj Singh was found missing after the previous invasion'- stated the letter.
Meenal’s father watched her cry and left the place secretly.

Another time, but Meenal wrote a letter, gave it to her father for sending them to her beloved Suraj yet again as if nothing ever happened, keeping all the pain to her heart and pretending to be a stranger to the state of affairs.
Until Suraj arrives, Meenal lives in the bliss of letter...

synopsis:
Famous businessman G.S Singhaniya's ship sinks in the   sea.                                                                                                                       This causes a loss of 100 crores to G.S Singhaniya Due to this loss, his house car company comes to be auctioned.Upset G.S Singhaniya arrives at the form house wiith his assistant Jagdish and his son Dev.Leaving his son Dev with Jagdish and goes to arrange the money.Here Jagdish reaches home with Dev and waiting for Singhaniya but it night and Singhniya does not come back at home.To find Singhniya, Jagdish leaves Dev at home and leaves at night.But he doesn't come back.Eventually Singhniya's son waits for his father and Jagdish but when both do not come, he leaves home alone in search of his father.When Dev comes out of the house,on the way he meets Kumbhi and Chandu,who are very big drug mafia.As soon as Dev introduces himself Chandu and Kumbhi alsotake him to his house.Since childhood Chandu and Kumbhi raise and raise Dev and after 20 years, Chandu and Kumbhi can work on Dev, which is the work of drug sumggling.On Dev's refusal, Chnadu and Kumbhi tell him about the gold and send him to take the goods.Eventually Dev comes to know about the drug smuggling of both of them and Dev rebels them.Dev burns the bag of the drug which is worth 10 crores.Dev asks top complain to the police,seeing this,Chandu and Kumbhi get furious and plan to kill Dev.In this plan,Chandu and Kumbhi send a girl to Dev, Dev brings her to his house to help the distressed girl,later it is learned that she had gone to rape the girl in the rape case.Troubeld Dev leave Chandu and Kumbhi;s base,but when Sakshi learns that chandu and Kumbhiare the murderers of Dev's father,she tells him evrything on the phone,Dev does not believe at first, but Dev believes to Sakshi when she  says more. Eventually Dev takes revange for his father's murder by killing Chandu and Kuumbhi,thus ending a gang smuggler in Mumbai.
THe law will punish Dev for killing Chandu and KUmbhi,butDev saved the lives of millions of youth by elimiating the plantets....

title: We Have Ashes As Offerings : Nous Avons Des Cendres Pour Offrandes French Short film
synopsis:
We have ashes as offerings (2021) is the first production of Lucas Tétry-Rivière, a young artist from the Indian Ocean, born in Réunion.
Currently living in Paris, they went back many times for three years to Bras-Panon, a small town located on the eastern side of the island, where their maternal grandmother lives. With only few resources (a camera and phone), they took the plunge and set for themself the goal of establishing their family’s first digital archive, through oral stories and the everyday life of their grandmother, Reine Tétry, born as a subject of the French State in 1925.

title: Kisa Gotami  - Hindi Drama Short film
synopsis:
Kisa Gotami was the wife of a wealthy man of Shravasti. Her story is one of the more famous ones in Buddhism. After losing her ony child, Kisa Gotami became desperate and asked if anyone could help her. Her sorrow was so great that many thought she had lost her mind. An old man told her to see the Buddha. The Buddha told her that he could bring the child back to life if she could find a fistful of mustard seeds from a family where no one has ever died. She desperately went from house to house, but to her disappointment, she could not find a house that had not suffered the death of a family member. Finally the realisation struck her that there is no house free from mortality. She returned to the Buddha, who comforted her and preached to her the truth. She was awakened and entered the first stage of enlightenment. Eventually, she bacame an Arhat.

title: Ruuposh - Urdu Documentary Short film
synopsis:
Ruuposh is an idiosyncratic documentary that features Ruksana Begum- her late father Mehboob Khan and her son- Mohd Fehmeed, who belong to a Muslim minority family of India 2021. The documentary follows a personal narrative of how Mehboob Khan stayed back in India at the time of partition while his entire extended family moved to Pakistan. Ever since, there had been an on and off contact with the relatives in Pakistan which completely ceased off a few decades ago. And while, the younger generation wants to establish contact with the parted relatives- does the political circumstances of the two countries allow the possibility and sustenance of any friendly relationship? The documentary is a larger comment on the current political state of India- it talks about the growing intolerance and the biased relationship the country continues to have with its separated neighbor even after years of independence.

title: Just Like Water - Greece Emotional Shortfilm
synopsis:
The movie is about the father of the director, Spyros, his life and experiences. He is the protagonist and narrator. The "Just Like Water" speaks about time and the circle of life.

The questions asked are, what is time and how do we perceive it? Do we really live the moments in our daily lives and do we give them their due value?

So, we follow a magical journey through time, embedded with vivid images of Spyros, grandfather, man, child ... A girl and a woman always there. A patchwork of images, accompanied by original musical composition, sketched in detail: it weaves the puzzle of a whole life nestled in the Island of Crete (Greece).

title: Rumors - English Drama Short film
synopsis:
Rumours is a film about memory, how memory works and how we save our memories.
I had a hard task ahead - visualise something abstract and created in this first half work by collecting more than 500 hours of video footage. These materials include surveillance videos, amateur street photography videos, documentaries and Actual shooting.
Most of our memories pass like time; they become vague memories of broken pieces and emotions.They can make us do great things or terrible things.
I tried this film, gives us a glimpse at how our brain works. we see a montage of video and audio, noise. Just like our brains, they manage to find a memory based upon a perfume we smell by passerby.
Memory is like a hybrid between physical and digital world.This only running at almost 7 minutes film gives us a glimpse at negative things in life can affect us.

title: Ashq - Urdu Emotional Short film
synopsis:
ASHQ “Tears” is the 1980s story of an independent middle-aged Pakistani filmmaker (Gulzar) whose film is shortlisted – the only one from South Asia, for Award and is invited by the London Film Committee at the Award ceremony. Since, Gulzar is unable to join the ceremony due to his ailing mother’s scheduled operation during the same dates, the film committee decides to record his “winning speech” with a replica award in his hand, as an alternate way to show at the ceremony, in case Gulzar wins the Award.

A local senior film journalist (Irfan)who is hired and assigned the task by the committee, reaches Gulzar’s old fashioned home in the walled city of Lahore to record his speech. Irfan is not satisfied with the first take and wants retake with emotions of intense happiness in Gulzar’s speech. He tries to convince Gulzar that a “winning speech” must have tears of joy to thrill the audience. An unconvinced Gulzar tells Irfan that, for him, still there is long way go to win the Award and without winning in reality, it is hard for him to shed tears of joy in the Recording.

Puzzled, but determined Irfan keeps insisting Gulzar to go deep down his memory lane and recall the saddest moments of his life, powerful enough to bring tears to his eyes. “Tears are Tears – it makes no difference, be these bring by happiness or sadness”, Irfan finally convinces Gulzar.

Retake starts, Gulzar gives an impressive “winning speech” bursting out with tears rolling down his cheeks, making Irfan and his cameraman standing stunned. Irfan is highly impressed with Gulzar’s performance and is very happy with the recording. After saying good bye to Irfan, an emotionally loaded Gulzar drops himself on an arm chair, listening a sad song on gramophone, sinks into deep thoughts.

Six months later his bedridden mother dies. His relatives, nears and dears wail and weep but an emotionless Gulzar is standing blank with dry eyes at the burial ceremony in his ancestral village. He suffers heavy toll of the special tears - wasted.

title: Lebenspanorama - English Documentary Short film
synopsis:
The majority of people believe in life after death, for example in the immortality of the soul or the resurrection of the dead. About 25% believe in reincarnation.
Cult and observation sites from the Bronze Age are witnesses that our ancestors already believed in life after death.
The question of what form the transformation of the spirit takes after death is now accompanied by research in the field of neuroscience, near-death experiences, reincarnation research, Buddhist belief and regression therapy. The film poses these questions to the Buddhist teacher Ole Nydahl, the psychologist Hans Markowitsch and the reincarnation researcher Dieter Hassler. Will the new insights bring us certainty?

title: Til The Orchid Blooms - Chinese Drama Short film
synopsis:
Fenglan, a mother of two young adults spends a day at the beach with her harmonious family. Running on the golden sand, playing in the water and collecting seashells, the beautiful day ends in a picture perfect moment. Little does she know that the kind wishes they send each other manifest in a completely different manner shortly thereafter.

Fenglan’s daughter is a practitioner of Falun Dafa and believes in the principles of Truth, Compassion, and Forbearance. Only in China are such people persecuted for their beliefs. Seeing her daughter illegally arrested by the police, Fenglan suffers from amnesia since that traumatic event. Flashbacks of the past intertwine with her current confused state, tearing apart the family.

A shocking truth revealed by her son sinks Fenglan into the depths of consciousness. Wondrous events begin happening as a dream blends into reality, and the light starts returning to her life.

Nalini is a novelist and she moves into her new rented apartment to write her novel. She meets Varun at a party and they soon move in together. Moving through the seasons, Nalini explores the cycle of life with experiencing youthful love , anger, violence, depression and finally acceptance and peace. The film ends with a new beginning and a new cycle, implying an endless repetition with subtle variation.

title: I Am Ramdeen  Hindi
synopsis:
‘I am Ramdeen’ is a stop motion animated short based on the issue of death penalty in India. The film is a visual representation of a poem written by Jahnavi Misra. It aims to highlight the fact that the simple violence of punitive measures like capital punishment often belies systemic inability or refusal to deal with deeper societal problems like poverty and inequality.

The film shows a death row prisoner named Ramdeen, celebrating on finally receiving a verdict on his case, after waiting for twenty years. It is a fully handmade animated film and the materials used are terracotta clay and paper.

The short is written by Jahnavi Misra and is animated by Apoorva Mundoor and Jahnavi Misra. Voices are by Ramashanker Misra and Lokendra Sharma.

title: Collar  Russian Drama
synopsis:
Olechka's husband controls Her in everything, they live according to his laws, lead an ascetic lifestyle. The girl feels that she is happily married. But everything changes when a naked neighbor breaks into their quiet and measured life. It spoils Olechka's collar - the only decoration of her black wardrobe. The husband throws out the damaged collar and sends his wife to buy a new one. In the store, she meets the same neighbor, who turns out to be a consultant. A neighbor sells Olechka an unusual collar, the girl is convinced that the collar has power over her. She is torn between the image of the "Madonna" dictated by her husband and the image of the" harlot " to which her collar pushes her. The collar inclines the girl to change her husband, but Olechka resists the desires of the collar and breaks it. At this moment, the girl understands the terrible: the collar does not have magic power and all this time she acted on her own.

title: Cities Of Ladies - Silent Animation Documentary Short film
synopsis:
Cities of Ladies" is an immersive poetic animation documentary trilogycreated through multi-disciplinary art practices to foster hope, change and promote self-development; through the exploration and recognition of personal views and ideologies.

The trilogy comprises of 3 short film
Cities of Ladies 1 (WE) Flat Screen
Cities of Ladies 2 (YOU) 360 VR
Cities of Ladies 3 (I) 360 VR

Inspired by “The Book of the City of Ladies” by Christine de Pizan (1364 – c. 1430), the film has been created in two formats: one in traditional flat screen and the other in 360 monoscopic. The film has a total of three versions. One flat screen version and two 360 versions. Each version captures a different stage of the process of thinking, making, watching and feeling the collection of voices, sharing their thoughts about today’s world.

Cities of ladies was produced with support from Arts Council England’s Emergency Response Fund, Animate Projects Arts, in association with QUAD Derby and Phoenix, Leicester.

title: Freudian Slip - Tamil Drama Short film
synopsis:
The short film is based on 'Free At Last', a Tamil story by Ashokamitran, translated by N Kalyan Raman. The film set in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, revolves around a couple who has passed out of Drama school and is having a hard time finding work, money and time for each other. Chander, the protagonist, finds himself irritated and angry at his reality - he projects that anger on Sujatha.
Sujatha is making her ends meet by working as a masseuse for Miss SSP, who is in the news these days for putting a sexual harassment allegation against Ramanujan, a powerful politician in Chennai. Ramanujan's son Ashwin happens to be Sujatha's college friend and tries helping her and Chander to get a role in his friend Siddharta's film, who happens to be the new young talented director everyone's talking about.

title: Harichalum Gunichalum Onnu - Malayalam Drama Short film
synopsis:
The I and the mine vanish.
I see myself on the mirror.
But the portrait is not mine.

Face filled with grey hairs,
Wrinkles on the temple
The eyes, withdrawn to their holes
And pale, due to fear,
Implore at me with folded hands.

They come to my neck, in the shape of a bitch just littered, and squeeze it.

While pelting stones
When wearing crown of thrones,
The blood flow does not show any sign of pain,Rather they reflect as a wonderful piece of art.

The excruciating pain, caused by lack of air, did not affect the agonising pain of my life.

I walk swiftly,
Carrying him...

title: Blizzard - Persian Drama Shortfilm
synopsis:
Blizzard; It is a human rights film that tells the story of a woman who loses her hunter husband in a sea storm. She stays with her only child who is seriously ill. woman to save her son from a disease due to Iran's high inflation and the words of the Iranian Minister of Health that he can not spend 1 billion toman a year to keep these patients alive for 2 more years. It caused the drug to rise sharply. The woman herself had to try different ways to keep her son alive and sometimes stood in front of the representatives of the Iranian government. Because he had no money to pay them as a bribe, and they stopped him from working and threatened to destroy his workplace. Eventually, his son died due to outdated and corrupt drugs and they ruined the woman's workplace.

title: Оvertime  Russian Award winning Shortfilm
synopsis:
A football player, forward of FC “Molniya” Bulat Tagirov is a man of strong principles and is uncompromising in his convictions. He does not show indulgence either to himself or others.
Recently, he has lost his elder brother, with who he could not reconcile for a long time because of misunderstanding in terms of conscience, religion, but, most importantly, because of what happened in their family. Meanwhile, the football community discusses the news that the time of the “legendary Tagirov” in big football came out, and he will be replaced by a more promising player.
Prompted by the desire to prove that he is the best and to compensate for his painful loss with a victory, Tagirov goes to the match with the local football team as if it was the battle. But he has to face a new test of his resistance.
Bulat breaks the connection with everything that was dear to him: people he is closest to, the team, he does not dare to go to mosque. Just like a player in overtime, he must realize who he needs to make a decisive pass to. But this time the action takes place not on the pitch, but in life ...

title: Realisation - Marathi Drama Short film
synopsis:
Some feeling regarding some person or something which give realisation of that favour is known as JANEEV. It is an emotional story of Aanand who has dedicated all his life towards his ailing mother. He does a lot of adjustments & sacrifices in his life to keep his mother happy. His office boss & other friends sometimes ridicule him & make fun of him being a Mamma's boy. Aanand ignores them & works hard to balance his professional & personal life. His 'never say die' positive attitude towards life brings lots of changes in his life journey. One day his Boss out of curiosity visits Aanand's house & gets shocked when he witnesses the real truth of Aanand's life at home. The story brings out an Emotional Realisation towards life & people.

title: Captain - Russian Drama Short film
synopsis:
It's war again. A checkpoint on the border of an unknown empire. There is no sign of war and it seems like there's no danger. The captain, recently arrived from the front, orders the soldiers to shoot at all suspicious people who cross the border. Soldiers carry out his order. But once they receive an order from the headquarters to ban the execution of civilians. And at this time through the post pass two young men who have a map, and the old man. It seems to the captain that the young men are spies, and the old man looks so strange ... The soldiers remind him of the order. But the captain still gives the order for the execution. He is sick. At night, an old man comes to him and reminds who we really are. The old man - Cain - the first killer. He is his conscience, his torment. The next morning the soldier tells the captain that all men shot. He understands that the old man came to him in delirium. The captain is confused. He leaves the checkpoint.

title: After Amal - Bengali Drama Short film
synopsis:
In anthropology lineage is often determined in more than one way. “Genitor “ is the name for the “physical” father (in many instances biological), while “Pater” is the social father. While in many contexts they are one, but in others they can refer to different individuals. In this process of birth affirmation-- patrilineage is preserved within the family, and the voice of the woman remains obscure. Unless....

This is the untold story of many women who go through life trying to find an outlet. This is one such nightmare turned into a fairy tale in a period called now....

title: Cinema Mudo - Silent Short film
synopsis:
Children playing and having fun create a projector from a burnt-out lamp, a shoe box and a piece of mirror.

The “Cinema Mudo” project is the achievement of a short film and at the same time work together with a community living in a high-risk area. All children (actors and actresses) who work in the movie are residents in the community Vila Torres, an at-risk area in Curitiba. They were chosen after a theater classes for children held in that community as part as the film project.

The story shows three children (two boys and a girl), in the mid-thirties, setting up a movie frame projection room. They build a projector with an empty shoe box with two holes with the size of a film frame. Inside the box they put a burnt-out bulb without its filament and filled with water. This bulb is used as a lens. Children had to wait until the bulb burned out. Those electrical bulbs were rare in those days.

Children visit the city's cinema, dig through the trash in the screening room and collect pieces of film for their projections. When the children are going to do the test with the projector, the girl, in charge of filling the bulb with water, let it fall and the bulb breaks. The boys are not happy. After dreams and expectancies, the girl gets another bulb and the projector is ready.

On the day of the presentation they invite friends to watch the presentation. The sun does not appear and the children are distressed. But, at last, the sun and the light appear and the projection is made.

In a dark room a ray of light passes between two tiles. With a piece of mirror one of the children reflects the sunlight through one of the holes in the shoe box. The sunbeam passes through the frame, passes through the lamp with water and projects the image on the wall.

The short film proposes, as a background, to expose the history of cinema from the transition between silent and spoken cinema. This exhibition is made through the frames with references to the most representative films of the transition, covering the specific period between 1930 and 1934.

The photography of the film is based on films from this period directed by filmmakers such as Humberto Mauro, Charles Chaplin, Ardeshir Irani, Mário Peixoto, Carl Theodor Dreyer, Yasujiro Ozu, Josef von Sternberg, Luiz Buñuel, Kenji Mizoguchi, Jean Vigo, Cecil B. DeMille, Mark Fric, Carl Lamac Friedrich Wilhelm Murnau, Zhang Shichuan, Yakov Protazanov, Victor Sjöstrom, René Clair, W. S. Van Dyke, Leitão de Barros, René Clair, Aleksandr Dovzhenko, Sergei Eisenstein, Orestes Laskos, Alessandro Blasetti, Manuel de Oliveira, Jean Renoir and Fritz Lang.

It is known that with the advent of sound in cinema, which occurred in the years 1926/1927, some directors resisted the new technology. City Lights, by Charles Chaplin, performed in 1931, four years after The Jazz Singer, was still mute. The Vampire, by C. Th. Dreyer, performed in 1932, followed Chaplin's resistance. Yasujiro Ozu made his first spoken film only in 1936. His compatriot Mizoguchi had already experienced sound in his first film “Fujiwara Yoshie no Furusato”, in 1930.

In L’Atalante, 1934, Jean Vigo, despite being his 3rd. sound film, preserves the structure of a silent movie. The character Jean after losing his beloved Juliette, remains 23 minutes without speaking in the film. The sequence is a “silent film” within the “spoken film”.

Cecil B. DeMille, one of the founders of Paramount Pictures Corporation and, therefore, having the technology available, appropriates the sound only in Madame Satam, from 1932. Sjöstrom only debuted his first spoken film in 1930, A Lady to Love. When he returned to Sweden in 1937, he still made two silent movies.

In Ganga Bruta, from 1933, Humberto Mauro used some dialogues, but the film is essentially silent. In Limite, 1931, Mário Peixoto, in addition to making a silent film, uses only a small dialogue with signs, making the film absolutely silent. That same year Ardeshir Irani directed India's first spoken (and sung) film, Alam ara. Still in 1931, Leitão de Barros made his first spoken film in Portugal, with a screenplay by René Clair: A severa.

That year, in Russia, Dovzhenko performed Terra, still silent. The father of Russian cinema Yakov Protazanov had made his first sound film a year earlier, The Miracle of Saint George. The Vasilyev brothers started their careers in 1930 with a silent film Spyashchaya Krasavitsa. In 1932 they made Lichnoe Delo, a mixture of silent and sound cinema. Only in 1934 did they make a truly talked-about film, Chapaev. In that year Manuel de Oliveira made his first cinematographic work, the short documentary (silent) Douro, Faina Fluvial. Sergei Eisenstein had already experienced sound in cinema, but when he made ¡Qué viva México!, in 1932, he preferred, however, to make it silent. Vsovolod Pudovkin's transition film was The Defector in 1933. A year earlier, the wizard of silent film editing Kulechov started his sound films with “The Horizon”.

Mark Fric directed with Carl Lamac in 1931, in Czechoslovakia, “On a Jeho Sestra”, the 1st. spoken film of the directors. Josef von Sternberg made in 1929 Thunderbolt, a film with a double version: one spoken and the other silent. In 1930 he made Blue Angel, this one appropriating the sound fully.

Fritz Lang and Jean Renoir made their first films spoken in 1931 “M” and “La Chienne”, respectively, incorporating sound as a cinematographic language. The same had already done Hitchcock with Blackmail and confession, 1929, his first spoken film. But, Hitchcock, that same year, still made the silent film The Manxman.

Of the 55 films directed by the Greek Orestes Laskos, two were made in the 1930s, both silent. The first was Dafnis and Cloé in 1930. In Italy, Alessandro Blasetti and Mario Camerini began their cinematographic careers with a silent film from 1929. In 1930 Blasetti directed Nerone, the first spoken. The first spoken film in Mexico was “Más Fuerte que el Deber”, directed by Alexandro J. Sevellia, in 1931. The Italian-Argentine Mario Parpagnoli, who directed 30 films, made his last, still silent, in 1930, the musical drama “Adiós Argentina”.

In 1930, Zhang Shi Chan made China's first spoken film: Sing-song Red peony. In that year Boreslaw Newloyn made his only film and the first spoken film in Poland: The Moral of Mrs. Dulska. In the same year, in Denmark, George Schneevoigt performed Eskimo, already spoken.

Many directors have not adapted to the advent of sound, some have ended their careers. Others incorporated sound into their films as part of their cinematic language. After all, as Béla Balázs said, cinema is the only art in which silence is possible.

In addition to direct references to these directors, there are many references to Russian director Andrei Tarkovski. These are, sometimes in the image, sometimes in the audio.

title: Nyayam Vijayathe - Kannada Suspense Thriller Short film
synopsis:
The story starts with a man (named Adi) sitting and lighting a cigarette in front of 3 gunny bags filled with 3 human beings struggling to get out of the bag.
The 3 guys who are inside the bags and trying to move. Noticing this, Adi, comes to them and pours kerosene on them and gets ready to burn them alive.

Now the flash back, which is on that particular day starts to play.
Adi wakes up to the alarm tone. It's 3am. He starts getting ready and wears of police uniform and just when he is about to leave his house, his sister, who is lighting a diya, stops Adi and asks him if the justice will be delivered at least today. Adi gaves a little hope to her and prepares to leave.

Adi now enters the central jail with a hope of hanging the 3 convicts who have committed rape and a brutal murder of a lady who works as a nurse in a private hospital. After entering a particular section of the jail, he notices that the police constable who is in charge of the hanging, is now getting ready to head back home. Adi questions the police constable as to what is the reason for them to not perform the hanging.
Police constable, swamy, explains that one of the convicts purposely cut his hand in order to delay the hanging. Inspector Adi becomes furious after he gets to know this and rushes to the convicts cell in anger.
The convicts there mock a Adi regarding the committed crime. Triggered by this, Adi gets inside the cell and starts beating them up. It will be later interrupted by constable swamy and then Adi heads back home with frustration around 5 am.
Adi closes his eyes to recall what exactly had happened 5years ago.
In the flash back, Adi receives a call from his sister saying that, priya has agreed to go for a night shift instead of her (Asha) as she would have to stay at home for personal reason.
Flash back ends and Adi enters his house where his sister, Asha, will be waiting for him to bring in a good news of justice being provided to priya, but helpless Adi doesn't respond and shuts his room's door.

The same morning around 8am Adi is woken up by a call from constable swamy.
Swamy informs Adi that the officer bhaskar has asked Adi to meet him in officer's farm house. Adi agrees to it.

Bhaskar and swamy arrive at the farm house while Adi is waiting for them. As soon as bhaskar gets out of the car, he lashes out at Adi for beating up the convicts inside the cell and explains they have high political influence and should not be treated that way though it is proven that they are only the convicts. Adi feels pathetic about the system and expresses his views on the judicial system and how it is being helpful for the convicts to escape even after they are proven guilty.
Meanwhile, Adi, also explains what exactly had happened the night the girl was murdered and how she was tortured and finally dead.
Bhaskar finally concludes the conversation by telling Adi that those 3 convicts will not be hanged and the documents will be ready stating that they were juvenile at the time of committing the crime. And they will be out if jail in a few years.

Devastated and shocked Adi doesn't know what to do and now Bhaskar comes to him and tells and Adi should himself, unofficially, take those three convicts for a medical treatment since they were hurt by Adi.

Now Adi plans to kidnap them and successfully does it and gets those 3 convicts into gunny bags.
Now the scene movies to the initial screenplay of the movie where 3 convicts are in the gunny bag struggling.
Once Adi throws the fire on them, he starts laughing and then breaks down recalling his past which is 5 years ago.

Now the flash black starts to play on screen, where Adi and his fiancee (priya)are hanging out in a deserted place. That's when Adi gets a call from his sister telling him that priya is not ready to go for a night shift instead of her. Adi asks her to check with her again after sometime. Meanwhile, Adi prepares to convince Priya, who is also Asha's friend, to go for a night shift for one night instead of his sister. Finally Priya agrees but on one condition. Condition being, Adi informs their parents about his and Priya's relationship to their parents the next day which is on Priya's birthday. The flash back ends.

Now priya, who was going to night shift, instead of Asha, is raped and brutally murdered.

Adi had promised Priya that those 3 convicts have to punished and help all the girls gain courage through act though their loved ones aren't with them at all times.

Adi now, cries after burning the rapists alive while he recalls what he had promised Priya while she was on a deathbed.

The end.

title: Everything finds it's limit in it's own physics - Silent Animation Short film
synopsis:
How often do we wish we had another identity? Why do gestures and creativity differ in every individual? Can an iron wire perceive itself as something else or will it remain just an iron wire?

Wie oft wünschen wir uns eine neue Identität? Warum unterscheiden sich Gesten und Kreativität in jedem Individuum? Kann ein Draht von sich aus etwas anderes empfinden, oder wird es nur ein Draht bleiben?

Quante volte vorremmo avere un'altra identità? Perchè gestualità e creatività si distinguono in ogni individuo? Può un filo di ferro percepirsi altro da se o resterà solo un filo di ferro?

title: The Breakfast - Tamil Political Fiction Short film
synopsis:
Storyline: Ganesh, a respected politician, and his wife Saritha are having breakfast together. Saritha has just returned from a trip, so Ganesh has prepared her favourite dish. While they are eating, Ganesh tells Saritha about all the things that happened during her absence. But she doesn't seem to listen. Something is bothering her. Saritha asks her husband a question. From this point on, the conversation turns into a revelation of the darkest secrets of their lives.

Director Biography - Varunagini

Varunagini was born in a small town in Sri Lanka, but was brought up in Germany, where she went to school and later studied pedagogy at the Goethe-University in Frankfurt/Main.

Stories in form of books and movies have always been a passion of hers. She started writing her own short stories at very young age. In 2016, she directed her first own movie based on one of her screenplays called 'Her verdict'- a short drama about a rape victim in India.

Filmfestival Awards: Jaipur International Filmfestival - Top 2nd Short Fiction

title: Alex & Nadia Forever - Russian Love Short film
synopsis:
Drenched in guilt and longing to his beloved Nadia, who was murdered by an unknown killer, Alex secludes himself at home while her family mourn her in their home. Mysterious mail deliveries to Nadia often arrive, easing his loneliness. Old videos of them on his phone make him miss her until it hurts. Out of the house windows he can see the woods where she was murdered and it hunts his mind. As a coping mechanism, Alex starts to slowly take on her identity. First he puts her jewelry on, then cuts his hair like her and eventually puts on her yellow dress. Slowly he starts redecorating the house, covering the walls with paintings of the smiley faces she used to love. In his despair, Alex decides to go out to the infernal woods and attempt to end up with the same fate as Nadia’s, but the unknown man who appears from the darkness passes over him. Eventually Alex decides to go share his sorrow with her family, while looking like her. At first they don’t know how to react to his bizarre appearance but the hardship and sorrow force them to allow him some room.

title: Kadaisi Echarikai - Tamil Social Awarness Short Film
synopsis:
This social awarenes film is about a normal human facing trouble in daily consuming foods, with toxic pesticides mixing items.
one morning the lead roller think to brushing his teeth with paste, but he knowing the fluoride effects,so he avoid .Then he going to drink a tea , suddenly he knows toxic content of the teapowder and sugar,again he avoid.After that he like to buying some vegetables & fruits, after a minute he hears the pesticides spraying method of plant cultivation ,so he dropped the plan.that day afternoon he feeling not well, so he taking some tablet but that also expired &highly dosage tablet.he feel wexed.At night he feeling hungry to eat some parrota in wheel barrow.at the minute he knowing the toxic content of parrota making powder maida.In maida had a Alaskhan, this is chemical content of sugar experiment in rat,so he skiped.Finally he decided to drinking water but water having non balanced PH value and chemical.Finally he giving the message to society,In ancian time the food contamination is about the level of only soil stones and all, but we all healthy,but today world all the food contains toxic and pesticides ,it's dangerous to leading life.which is the reason for lot of people having sugar pressure, cancer and dangerous diseases.The worst politics and worst commercial system is the reason for this dangerous situations.so we all back to ancian food habbit and organic life. its necessary for saving healthy life and living organisms.AIM OF THIS FILM-we all should turn to ancient (traditional )organic life style.

title: Work From Home - Hindi Emotional Shortfilm
synopsis:
Storyline: This sudden Lockdown has thrown many a lives off balance financially, emotionally and psychologically. Spending 2-3 months confined in the four walls of our homes, in a way, has been what we always wished for, a long holiday. Work From Home has become the new normal but this has uncovered some stark realities. Many couples have rediscovered their bond, some have realized they cant bear to see each other any more and other relationships have taken a more dangerous turn.

Work From Home, is a short film written and produced during this, once in a century, lockdown era. It deals with the intricacies of the life of a couple who have 2 kids and are married for over a decade, but soon enough, the cracks in their relationship show up and then life takes a sudden turn.

A seemingly busy husband, a tormented and neglected wife and another woman. Will this be the end of this relationship or a new beginning of a happier life…. The film has been written , shot, edited during the Lockdown and each actor has worked from their own respective homes

title: Pillow Nothing But Life - Malayalam Drama Short film
synopsis:
Storyline: Pillow is a travel of a man through his memories through his all time companion - pillow. All the key events happened in his life was completely connected with a pillow. Now, in his mid 20s, he is fed up with this life and is decided to leave this world. He is all set to bid adieu. During these last minutes he is going through his memories. But something is hooking him back! What will happen in his life? This forms the crux of the story.

Directed by: Francis Joseph Jeera

Francis Joseph Jeera, the director of the movie, is an experienced adfilm- documentary maker based out in Kerala. After a short stint as an assistant director, post media studies, he shifted his focus to call shots for documentaries and commercials. In distinct intervals he associated in some films and the last in the list is recent hit in Netflix - Kappela, in which he is the associate director.

title: Sachin Live From Pune - Marathi Emotional drama short film
synopsis:
Sachin is a hardworking teenager with a big dream that one day he will make his mark in the world of cricket like a legendary cricket player Sachin Tendulkar - whom he is named after. Sachin lives with poor family in a metro slum area of Pune. He has an immense love for cricket but can't afford professional coaching. He finds a way to train himself. He watches other kids practicing under coach and learn from it.

One day, at inter-school cricket competition, he impresses the selection committee and gets a chance to show his skills in All India Under-18 Team Selection process. But destiny has planned something else for him.   Sachin Live from Pune is a universal story of how minor sport injuries which teenagers choose to hide in fear of financial burdens on parents, could affect their dreams, sometimes the life.

title: Nainsi - Hindi Experimental shortfilm
synopsis:
This is the story of an innocent, underprivileged daughter of Bundelkhand region, of Madhya Pradesh State, in the heartland of India. Nainsi had a rash on her forehead since her childhood, that gradually grew into a large tumor. It caused a lot of trouble and embarrassment to her and her family.
But, in such a time of utter despair, Dr. Ajay Lall and Dr. Mrs.Indu Lall, founders and directors, Aadharshila Sansthan, CICM Damoh, who are always ready to serve the suffering communities in the society, along with their family members, came forward and extended their helping hands.
A great miracle took place!They not only added colors in the colorless life of this innocent daughter, but by providing means for the treatment of Nainsi's cancerous tumor on her forehead, they also gave new gifts of hope and happiness to her family.

A girl, who never went out of her province, goes to America with her mother for treatment!
She reaches to Dallas, Texas,Where the surgery is performed.
More than Six Hundred Thousand Dollars ($600,000) are spent on the Operation.
And all the travel expenses are borne by the organization and ministry partners.
Now She has fully recovered.
This short film has become entertaining as well as leaving a message for the society to help the poor people.

title: Howdy Foody -  hindi drama shortfilm
synopsis:
The unprecedented Corona period, which unfortunately still continuing, brought all-round inexplicable detrimental effects in human lives. So many rarest of rare and weird experiences shockingly surprised people across the globe, who somehow survived.
The digital information systems flooded with guessed lifesaving and life teaching hacks, but, still there were lot of real life stories born out of disguise.
“Howdy Foody”, as a Story, born out of unintentional cavalier behavior of us, the survived people amid Corona struck dystopian world.
The story revolves around the unique importance of the Food and its journey, and, attract our attention for the needful approach towards the burning issue.

title: YOUR CALL - MALAYALAM shortfilm
synopsis:
“YOUR CALL” is a simple yet dramatic short film with just one main character. YOUR CALL is an attempt to tell about a young man's choice of lifestyle in a big metro city, that leads to some unpleasant outcomes and eventually some bitter lessons learnt from those intense life moments. “Decisions that you take, Determines your Destiny”. Sometimes, one’s wrong or irresponsible decision or choice could end up in a bad destiny for self and the others around. Whatever is that destiny or negative outcome, it was always YOUR CALL in the first place. So it’s very important to think really wisely while taking YOUR CALL. That’s the major life message that we are trying to deliver through our small film along with some other related social messages.

title: "WHO AM I" - Hindi drama shortfilm
synopsis:
The hearts that have danced to the joy of their own celebrations, the souls that have seen the bonds of love grow, the words that have brought our minds together, this joy is also engulfed with the pain of being in exile, the tears of moving away from our own land, the sadness of facing the exodus, we Kashmiri Pandits have still joined hands together to revive our language, culture traditions and literature. We as a community where each and every one of us face the identity crisis, "WHO AM I" have still been working to pass our literature culture language and traditions to our next generation.
As a filmmaker, As a Kashmiri pandit, I Sanjay Raina have lived each and every moment of pain and happiness and each and every shot of the film is a depiction of what I feel and what my brother's and sisters across the globe feel. Who am I is just not three words, it is just not the pain of three generations, it is the pain of about seven generations and perhaps many more generations to come ..depicted in the style where every frame, every shot speaks of our lives, the voices you hear are heartfelt pain and joys, the music you listen to is all about our moving on in life.. but still one question will always haunt us ..who am I, an experimental film that shows how we Kashmiri Pandits have revived our language, culture, traditions and literature. "WHO AM I" is my humble attempt to weave together the real-time stories, interviews of poets, social activists, media professionals, actual visuals of literature, culture language and traditions collected over a period of time, to showcase , to bring out the pains and gains of Kahsmiri Pandits.

Sanjay Raina :  Director
Sanjay Raina  : Writer
Sanjay Raina, Manav Films : Producer

Key Cast:
Jitin Raina 
Rashi Koul

title: Paanpoi - Hindi Childrens Oriented Short Film
synopsis:
A devout, god-fearing man, Patil resides in a small village in Maharashtra, with his wife Laxmi, daughter Padma and son Harish.
The village has no regular supply of drinking water. Before sunrise, the family goes to the river, where the father performs his prayers, while the wife and kids wash clothes and refill water pots. While returning home, the father continues his prayers, while the wife and kids carry water pots on their heads.
Patil has set up a Paanpoi stall (a setup erected for supplying water to passersby) on the road between their home and kids' school to earn the blessings of the gods. However, there are two rules of the paanpoi: One - The water must be fetched bare feet and Two - Patil's family cannot drink his holy water which is reserved for passersby. If any member drinks this, they can be cursed, and all their efforts will go in vain.
On a hot sunny afternoon, Padma with Harish on the pillion, are eating leftover spicy food while cycling back from their school. Harish urgently needs water as he starts coughing while eating, but their water bottles are empty. Padma speeds the bicycle towards their home, but they skid and fall near the Paanpoi.
Now, they must decide whether to drink the cool forbidden water of the Paanpoi and break their father's dictates or walk back home in the hot sun, thirsty, pushing the broken bicycle.

title: Boomerang -  hindi drama shortfilm
synopsis:
This film is an adaptation of a story called THE SHROUD written by India’s most famous and respected writer Munshi Premchand. It is the story of two poor characters Ghisu and Madhav who are very lazy. Ghisu is the father and Madhav is the son. Both just relax and rest all the time. They don’t go for work until they have nothing to eat. Wife of Madhav, Kamla is pregnant and is in labor pain. They both have no money for her treatment. Next morning, she dies due to the lack of treatment. After the death they have to arrange money for her funeral. They beg money from some landlords and neighbors and collect some amount. They go to buy a Shroud for her funeral in a city nearby but before purchasing the shroud they spend the money for drink and eat roasted chicken. Rest of the money is looted by some corrupt policemen. It’s late in the night. They both are drunk. Father becomes philosopher and tells his son insignificance of life and of course all the customs after death including putting a shroud on the corpse, which will ultimately burn with the body.
Next day when they wake up late in the morning, they both notice a baby girl left near them in the night. They both exchange looks and both are confused about this boomerang in their lives and the story ends.

title: Milestone - Marathi Suspense Short Film
synopsis:
For any crash or accident, the major causes may be 'driver/human', 'road infrastructure' or 'vehicle-related factors'. Sometimes, it can be a combination of these factors that can lead to accidents. Among these key factors, 'driver/human'-related factors or errors predominate. Examples of such human/driver factors include — "rash driving, fatigue/sleepiness, use of digital devices such as mobile phones while driving, alcohol." The short film "MileStone" was scripted with this in mind as it plays a major role in the death toll.
Although this story is fictional, it parallels events that happen or hear in all of our lives. The hero of this story is warned beforehand by his wife about "alcohol and mobile" while driving. But the hero ignores it and dies accidentally. Even after death, his drunkenness does not subside quickly and his soul dramatically remembers that he has died. But when he realizes, it is too late and he is very sad. He starts crying. He realizes his mistake. He prays to God and comes out of the nightmare.

title: Until 23 - English Drama Short film
synopsis:
Shina (22), an only child of Iya and Baba has lived in his hometown, Abeokuta, all his life. He meets Adesewa, a girl from Lagos who pleads with him to accompany her to Lagos as she dreads driving alone at night. She promises to bring him back the next day. Shina obliges.

The next day, Adesewa tries in vain to wake Shina. She drives him back to Abeokuta where he's rushed to Adaifala's shrine.

In Adaifala's shrine, Iya and Baba are reminded of the vow they made 22 years ago when they had come to Adaifala for a child after many years of childlessness. They had agreed to share Shina with the gods for the first 23 years of his life, and he was not to leave Abeokuta during those years. On the second day of every month, he would fall into a deep sleep and only be awakened by a ritual performed before sunset to guide his spirit back to earth.
Several attempts to resuscitate Shina fails. Shina dies, unaware of the vows made on his behalf.

title: Painkiller - English Drama Short Film
synopsis:
Ronnie’s world comes crashing down, when his wife leaves him. The feeling of losing his wife and daughter is something he can’t wrap his head around. We witness the anger, frustration, depression and helplessness of an enraged loner who spews macho, insensitive and sociophobe bile. His decent into madness is narrated through voice-over and flashbacks and dreams. Violence is present in Ronnie’s stream of consciousness, most of it is a torrential rant of complaint, abuse and negativity driven by xenophobia. The film is as much inside his head so a great deal of what we see is filtered through Ronnie’s narration.

title: Hatheli - Hindi Drama short film
synopsis:
A tale of undefined bond between two poles apart people Keshav and Sandhya. The story revolves around a village, where Sandhya, with her daughter came at her maternal village after 4 years of her marriage. Keshav notices her while going for work and he gets frightened with the situation of Sandhya as he sees her withered face and senectitude skin. He recalls his days with Sandhya, when they both were having their best time together as friends. He realized if he could have expressed his feelings at the right moment, he could have saved her from changing completely and losing herself. Will Keshav dare to ask Sandhya for a second chance ? title: Orchha   Is Hardaul Alive
synopsis:
"The Film is a history based drama where a research scholar is overwhelmed to believe that all historical facts are not available in books and that long practiced traditions and belief system do hold significant historical evidences.
Hardaul, a well accepted God in many parts of India specifically in Orchha of Bundelkhand region was a brave historical personality of seventeenth century. The emperor Jehangir had rewarded his father Veer Singh Bundela with a mansab of four thousand for being an assassin of Abul Fazl. Later Veer Singh's eldest son Jujhar Singh succeeded the throne of Orchha but soon dispatched to Deccan leaving the province into the hands of his minor brother Hardaul. Jujhar's wife served Hardaul as wet nurse along with her own son.
After Jujhar's return rumours poisoned him to believe that his brother Hardaul has developed close intimacy with his wife. He ordered his wife to poison Hardaul to prove her chastity and loyalty. On hearing this Hardaul volunteered to gulp the poison which he did and sacrificed his life to prove the sanctity of this holy relationship.
It is believed that Hardaul still appears in this region. People offer the wedding invitation to his temple first in order to remain assured of fulfillment of all needs during marriage
"

title: Myth | Hindi | Comedy, Thriller, Suspense Short film
synopsis:
Myth is a psychological thriller comedy that delves into the intriguing world of dissociative 
identity disorder (DID). The movie revolves around Natasha, the lead actress, who grapples with her condition while leading a double life. On the surface, Natasha portrays herself as a confident and  glamorous persona in the social media world, trying her best to be the epitome of coolness. However,  her true self is vastly different from the carefully crafted online image.
Natasha's life revolves around her two best friends, Pragati and Mayank. While Mayank is deeply 
infatuated with Natasha, Pragati also harbors romantic feelings for him. This love triangle adds an 
element of tension and complexity to their friendship.
As Natasha seeks love and companionship, she stumbles upon someone through her Instagram 
profile, a mysterious guy named Rishabh. The two begin dating, and for a while, Natasha finds 
happiness and excitement in their relationship. However, the plot takes a dark turn when she starts 
suspecting that something is amiss about Rishabh.
Unraveling the mystery, Natasha discovers a shocking truth - the person she is dating, Rishabh, is 
already deceased. The revelation shatters her world and pushes her deeper into the realms of her 
dissociative identity disorder. As Natasha's sanity starts slipping, she must confront her inner 
demons and grapple with her own mind to discern reality from illusion.
Throughout Myth, the movie takes the audience on a rollercoaster ride, blurring the lines 
between truth and falsehood, sanity and delusion. The film sheds light on the complexities of mental 
disorders, particularly dissociative identity disorder, challenging the misconceptions and stereotypes 
surrounding it.
As Natasha's world unravels, Pragati and Mayank become her pillars of support, but their own 
feelings for each other create a compelling sub-plot, adding emotional depth to the narrative.
Myth is a thought-provoking and gripping tale that explores the thin line between perception 
and reality, love and obsession, and the resilience of the human mind in the face of adversity. It is a 
rollercoaster of emotions, blending elements of psychological thrills, comedy, and tragedy to deliver 
an unforgettable cinematic experience.

CAST & CREW:-
Director: Ankit Anand
Producer: Ankit Anand, Prashant Chibbar and Vinay Kumar
Prerna Gusain as Natasha 
Himesh Shahi Rajput as Rishav
Piyush Sharma as Mayank
Ekta Obroy as Pragati

synopsis:

He calls one of his friends ‘Steven’ who is a researcher and tells him the bad experience of last night. 
Steven tells that these are ‘FOGGERS’ and they are from Northern part of Europe. Justin is shocked and Steven says he knows what to do.
What did Steven do next...

After bearing many hardships and the trafficker's criticisms, it is time to give birth in a sensitive border situation where the police are around them. title: Uravin Mozhi - The Language Of Relationship - Tamil - Shortfilm
synopsis:
The Language of Relationship (Uravin Mozhi) is a filial love drama between a stressed-out workaholic single mother, Anjali and her neglected gameaholic kid, Kavin.

Anjali is a Software Engineer who works 24/7 to save her job. Kavin is her 7-year-old son who is addicted to playing games on his mobile phone.

Due to Anjali's busy work schedule, she often misses out on taking care of Kavin. Kavin longs for his mother's love, but he feels disappointed that she is always busy with work. As a result, he turns to playing games on his mobile phone to find comfort.

This short film explains how Anjali and Kavin eventually learn to understand each other and fall in love.

This heartwarming story highlights the importance of understanding in relationship, duties of parents and explores the awareness about the issue of game addiction among children.

The concept of a "Purananuru" poem [Sangam Period Tamil Literature] which explains the duties of parenthood is also quoted in this short film.

title: Behind The Silver Screen - Tamil - Pilot Film

Lacking the expertise in film making, Sathyamoorthy is forced to pledge his house to Anbarasu, a cine financier. Senthil fails miserably in delivering the film. What happens to his family? title: Watch Ponnana Nimidangal - Starring Good night Manikandan & Balasaravanan
synopsis:
The story opens with a flashback set in the midst of a bustling wedding hall, where we first meet Sandhya. An unusual incident forges a close bond between her and Kathir, and over time, their friendship gradually evolves into love. However, Kathir hesitates to confess his feelings, worried that expressing his love might ruin their relationship and burdened by his current financial insecurities.

Adding a lighthearted counterpoint to this unfolding drama is Thiru, Kathir’s roommate. Through his lively conversations and witty exchanges, Thiru not only supports the storyline but also injects humor and perspective into the narrative.

While the film unfolds against a backdrop of diverse and picturesque locations, it follows the familiar path of a one-sided love story filled with comedy, romance, sacrifice, and deep friendship. Yet, just when the audience believes they know what to expect, an unexpected twist at the climax turns everything on its head.

title: Theeranisi - Tamil Thriller Shortfilm
synopsis:
நிலா 10 வயது சிறுமி. தந்தையை பிரிந்திருக்கும் தாயோடு வளர்கிறான். எப்பொழுதும் தந்தையின் நினைவாக இருக்கும் நிலா ஒரு நள்ளிரவில் தாய்க்குத் தெரியாமல் தந்தையைப் பார்க கிளம்புகிறாள்.

அவள் செல்லும் வழியில் பிச்சைக்காரன் ஒருவன் தனக்கு கிடைத்த பிரியாணிப் பொட்டலத்தைப் பிரித்து உண்ணுகிறான். அவனைக் கண்டு பயந்த நிலா தயக்கத்துடன் அவனைக் கடந்து செல்கையில் அவன் அவளின் வாசனையை நுகர்ந்து நன்றாக சாப்பிடுகிறான்.

அவனைக் கடந்து சென்ற நிலா சிறிது தூரத்தில் பைக்கில் வந்த ஒரு கழுவை சந்திக்கிறாள். அவன் அவளை விசாரிக்கையில் அவளின் தனிமையையும் பாதுகாப்பில்லாமையையும் அறிந்து அவளை அப்பாவிடம் கொண்டுசேர்ப்பதாக நயவஞ்சமாக பேசி தன் இடத்துக்கு கூட்டிச்சென்று தவறாக நடந்து கொள்கிறான்.

அவ்விருவரையும் எப்படியோ தொடர்ந்து வந்த பிச்சைக்காரன் தான் வைதிருந்த ஆயுதத்தைக் கொண்டு கழுவைத் தாக்கி குழந்தையை காப்பாற்றுகிறான். அப்பொழுது தான் தெரிகிறது பிச்சைக்காரன் கழுவைப் பிடிக்க வந்திருந்த இரகசிய பொலீஸ் என்று.

Nurtured by his parents in a nature friendly environment, 14 year old Agstaya who has hemiplegic cerebral palsy is not addicted to mobile or gadgets. Agstaya tries to help Ridwik get over his addiction and saves his life with his pro-nature innovative ideas.

This motivates Ridwik to become a Chief Development Officer and through his environment centered approch for making pligrimage corridors, plays a part in changing the consumerist and tourism free centered system

title: Arjunaa - Hindi - Thriller - Shortfilm
synopsis:
"The film delves into the story of Arjun’s legendary bow, Gandeev, and its connection to Shivam, his descendant living in the year 2024.
As darkness looms over Earth, a terrifying demon named Naad rises, threatening to destroy the planet. Shivam, an ordinary young man unaware of his divine lineage, must embrace his destiny, wielding the power of Gandeev to protect humanity from annihilation.
Blending breathtaking action, timeless mythology, and a thrilling narrative, Arjuna promises to be a cinematic experience like no other."

title: Karpi - Shortfilm -Tamil - Drama
synopsis:
Vetri was a bright boy with a gift no one else had—he could see hidden challenges others couldn’t. While his classmates solved regular problems, Vetri faced invisible trials that tested his mind and spirit.

One day, a mysterious letter led him to a secret world of knowledge, where books spoke in riddles and equations came alive. To move forward in his studies, Vetri had to overcome fears, unlock hidden truths, and solve puzzles that existed beyond normal sight.

Though no one knew what he faced, Vetri grew wiser every day—because he learned to see what others could not.

title: JULI - Shortfilm - Odia - Thriller
synopsis: Bhua, a tribal vlogger from Odisha, runs toward the railway track as a train roars past, forcing him to stop. He pulls out his cell phone and begins vlogging, sharing stories of his life with warmth, humor, and sorrow. He talks about Juli, about loss, about justice. His words are laced with pain, yet he smiles through them. But beneath his casual storytelling, an unsettling weight lingers—something unspoken, something he carries within him.

title: Samaira - Hindi - Horror - Shortfilm
synopsis: Father returns from home after a hectic day, only to see his daughter missing. Is she really missing, or is there more than what meets the eye? Find out on SAMAIRA.

title: The Last President - Drama - English - Shortfilm
synopsis: In the year 2048, a deposed world leader is locked away for defying the rule of a global AI dictatorship. Isolated in a cold, sterile prison of the future, he begins to unravel strange visions—memories of past lives, buried truths, and a destiny far beyond human understanding. As the fabric of reality bends beneath the pressure of unchecked artificial evolution, he ascends into a higher state of consciousness—emerging as humanity’s last chance to rise above the machine age and reclaim its soul.

title: Rape - Hinidi - Drama - Shortfilm
synopsis:
RAPE is a story that captures a poignant moment when Vidya Bharti is confronted by her 8-year-old daughter’s innocent yet unsettling question: "What is rape?" after coming across the word in a newspaper. While Vidya struggles to provide a simple answer, it is through her daily experiences and routine encounters that she comes to truly grasp the deeper meaning of rape—one that extends beyond its legal definition to the harsh realities faced by women every day.

title: Bunk- Hindi - Drama - Shortfilm
synopsis:
"“Bunk” is a heartfelt short film that beautifully captures the spirit of college life through the story of four friends. Set against the backdrop of everyday classroom monotony, the film explores how a seemingly simple decision to skip a lecture becomes a turning point in their bond.

As the friends step outside the walls of their classroom, they also step into a space where laughter, conversations, and unplanned adventures unfold. What begins as just another act of bunking soon transforms into a journey of rediscovering friendship and creating moments that will be etched in their memories forever.

The film delicately highlights the essence of youth—carefree choices, the joy of breaking routine, and the magic of finding connections beyond textbooks. Through raw emotions and relatable experiences, “Bunk” reminds us that sometimes the best lessons in life happen outside the classroom.

With its slice-of-life narrative, vibrant characters, and nostalgic charm, “Bunk” is not just about skipping a class; it’s about embracing the little joys of togetherness, the laughter shared in unexpected places, and the timeless memories that define our college years."

title: Socrates - English - Thriller - Shortfilm
synopsis:
**“Socrates” is a chilling thriller-horror that follows Ahmad, Thomas, and a group of college students as their research into ancient history and folklore takes a deadly turn. What begins as an academic pursuit unearths an urban legend that proves all too real—a vengeful spirit bound to an intelligent African Grey parrot. The bird, known as Socrates, carries the wrath of an old slave fable and seeks retribution against the descendants of cruel oppressors.

Desperate for answers, the group turns to Moses, a 102-year-old descendant of the parrot’s original keeper. Moses warns them to rebury what should never have been disturbed, but by then, Socrates has grown too powerful. Mimicking voices, hunting its prey, and feeding on fear, the spirit proves nearly unstoppable. As the nightmare deepens, Ahmad and Thomas realize they may be trapped in a fight they cannot win.

Dedicated to horror legend Tony Todd, Socrates is a haunting tale of history, vengeance, and survival.

title: The Final Birthday - Tamil - Horror - Shortfilm
synopsis:
"She was a young woman full of dreams — until a drunk driver took her life. Her soul, restless and filled with fury, begins haunting those who consume alcohol. She isn't a ghost seeking help… she's a ghost delivering punishment.

On a rainy night, she comes across a boy celebrating his birthday alone. She senses alcohol on him and marks him as her next target. She follows him through the night — observing his broken heart, loneliness, and guilt over a past he couldn’t fix.

As the night unfolds, she watches him feed a stray dog, cry in silence, and talk to a photo of his late mother. For the first time, she hesitates. He isn’t like the others. He’s kind. He’s hurt. He’s human.

A strange connection forms between the living and the dead. He begins to feel an invisible warmth around him, like someone is watching over him. He smiles for the first time that night, thinking maybe he isn’t alone.

But then… the clock strikes midnight.

Her eyes turn cold again. This is not a love story. It was never meant to be.

She softly whispers,
""Happy Birthday… I AM With you""

And with one touch — she takes his soul.

He falls to the ground, still wearing a gentle smile, as if he had finally found peace. But it wasn’t peace… it was the final gift."
